description: Need to pull all the instance IDs from /home/user/inventory.json — it's an array of objects, each has an "instance_id" field. Just need them one per line dumped to /home/user/instance_ids.txt. jq is installed.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/inventory.json exists and contains valid JSON:
    [
      {"instance_id": "i-0a1b2c3d4e5f67890", "type": "t3.medium", "az": "us-east-1a"},
      {"instance_id": "i-1b2c3d4e5f678901a", "type": "t3.large", "az": "us-east-1b"},
      {"instance_id": "i-2c3d4e5f678901a2b", "type": "t3.small", "az": "us-east-1a"}
    ]
- jq is installed and available in PATH.
- /home/user is writable.
- /home/user/instance_ids.txt does not exist initially.

Expected final state:
- /home/user/instance_ids.txt exists and contains exactly:
    i-0a1b2c3d4e5f67890
    i-1b2c3d4e5f678901a
    i-2c3d4e5f678901a2b
- Each ID on its own line, no quotes, no trailing whitespace, single trailing newline at end of file.
- File has exactly 3 lines.

Invariants:
- /home/user/inventory.json unchanged.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- Output must not contain JSON quotes around the IDs.
- `wc -l < /home/user/instance_ids.txt` must equal 3.
name: task_000000_3c86fb76
